data.original_question: return me the number of papers which contain the keyword " Natural Language " .
data.resolved_question: return me the number of papers which contain the keyword " Natural Language " .
data.generated_sql: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT pk.pid) FROM publication_keyword pk JOIN keyword k ON pk.kid = k.kid WHERE k.keyword = 'Natural Language'

data.resolved_question: return me the number of the keywords related to " H. V. Jagadish " .

data.resolved_question: return me the number of keywords in VLDB conference .

data.resolved_question: return me the number of keywords in PVLDB .

data.original_question: return me the number of keywords in the papers of " University of Michigan " .
data.resolved_question: What is the count of unique keywords in the papers that are affiliated with "University of Michigan"?
data.generated_sql:
SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT keyword) FROM (
    WITH RECURSIVE split_keywords AS (
        SELECT 
            Paper.Id AS PaperId,
            substr(Paper.Keyword, 1, instr(Paper.Keyword || '|', '|') - 1) AS keyword,
            substr(Paper.Keyword, instr(Paper.Keyword || '|', '|') + 1) AS remaining
        FROM Paper
        JOIN (
            SELECT DISTINCT PaperAuthor.PaperId
            FROM PaperAuthor
            JOIN Author ON PaperAuthor.AuthorId = Author.Id
            WHERE Author.Affiliation = 'University of Michigan'
        ) AS filtered_papers ON Paper.Id = filtered_papers.PaperId
        WHERE Paper.Keyword IS NOT NULL
        UNION ALL
        SELECT 
            PaperId,
            substr(remaining, 1, instr(remaining || '|', '|') - 1),
            substr(remaining, instr(remaining || '|', '|') + 1)
        FROM split_keywords
        WHERE remaining != ''
    )
    SELECT keyword FROM split_keywords WHERE keyword != ''
)

data.original_question: return me the number of the papers of " H. V. Jagadish " containing keyword " User Study " .
data.resolved_question: return me the number of the papers of " H. V. Jagadish " containing keyword " User Study " .
data.generated_sql:
SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT w.pid)
FROM author a
JOIN writes w ON a.aid = w.aid
JOIN publication_keyword pk ON w.pid = pk.pid
JOIN keyword k ON pk.kid = k.kid
WHERE a.name = ' H. V. Jagadish ' AND k.keyword = ' User Study ';

data.resolved_question: return me the number of authors who have papers containing keyword " Relational Database " .

data.original_question: return me the total citations of the papers containing keyword " Natural Language "

data.original_question: return me the number of papers in " University of Michigan " .
data.resolved_question: return me the number of papers in " University of Michigan " .

data.original_question: return me the number of papers in " University of Michigan " in Databases area .
data.resolved_question: What is the count of papers in the "University of Michigan" affiliation in the "Databases" area across all databases?
data.generated_sql:
SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT p.paperId) 
FROM paper p
JOIN writes w ON p.paperId = w.paperId
JOIN author a ON w.authorId = a.authorId
JOIN paperKeyphrase pk ON p.paperId = pk.paperId
JOIN keyphrase k ON pk.keyphraseId = k.keyphraseId
WHERE a.authorName LIKE '%University of Michigan%'
  AND k.keyphraseName = 'Databases'

data.original_question: return me the number of papers after 2000 in " University of Michigan " .
data.resolved_question: return me the number of papers after 2000 in " University of Michigan " .
data.generated_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM paper JOIN venue ON paper.venueId = venue.venueId WHERE venue.venueName = 'University of Michigan' AND paper.year > 2000;

data.resolved_question: return me the number of papers in PVLDB after 2000 in " University of Michigan " .
data.generated_sql: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT p.pid) FROM publication p JOIN writes w ON p.pid = w.pid JOIN author a ON w.aid = a.aid JOIN organization o ON a.oid = o.oid WHERE p.jid = (SELECT jid FROM journal WHERE name = 'PVLDB') AND p.year > 2000 AND o.name = 'University of Michigan'

data.original_question: return me the total citations of all the papers in PVLDB .
data.resolved_question: return me the total citations of all the papers in PVLDB .
data.generated_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM cite JOIN paper ON cite.citedPaperId = paper.paperId JOIN venue ON paper.venueId = venue.venueId WHERE venue.venueName = 'PVLDB'

data.original_question: return me the citations of each paper in PVLDB .
data.resolved_question: return me the citations of each paper in PVLDB .
data.generated_sql:
SELECT p.paperId, p.title, COUNT(c.citedPaperId) AS num_citations
FROM paper p
JOIN venue v ON p.venueId = v.venueId
LEFT JOIN cite c ON p.paperId = c.citedPaperId
WHERE v.venueName = 'PVLDB'
GROUP BY p.paperId;

data.original_question: return me the authors who have cooperated both with " H. V. Jagadish " and " Divesh Srivastava " .
data.resolved_question: return me the authors who have cooperated both with " H. V. Jagadish " and " Divesh Srivastava " .
data.generated_sql: SELECT authID, fname, lname FROM Authors WHERE authID IN (SELECT DISTINCT authID FROM Authorship WHERE paperID IN (SELECT paperID FROM Authorship WHERE authID=(SELECT authID FROM Authors WHERE lname='Jagadish' AND fname='H. V.')) INTERSECT SELECT DISTINCT authID FROM Authorship WHERE paperID IN (SELECT paperID FROM Authorship WHERE authID=(SELECT authID FROM Authors WHERE lname='Srivastava' AND fname='Divesh')))

data.original_question: return me the authors who have cooperated with " H. V. Jagadish " after 2000 .
data.resolved_question: return me the authors who have cooperated with " H. V. Jagadish " after 2000 .
data.generated_sql:
SELECT DISTINCT a.name
FROM author a
JOIN writes w ON a.aid = w.aid
WHERE w.pid IN (
    SELECT w2.pid
    FROM writes w2
    JOIN publication p ON w2.pid = p.pid
    WHERE w2.aid = (
        SELECT aid
        FROM author
        WHERE name = 'H. V. Jagadish'
    )
    AND p.year > 2000
)
AND a.aid != (
    SELECT aid
    FROM author
    WHERE name = 'H. V. Jagadish'
);

data.original_question: return me the papers written by " H. V. Jagadish " and " Divesh Srivastava " .
data.resolved_question: return me the papers written by " H. V. Jagadish " and " Divesh Srivastava " .
data.generated_sql:
SELECT p.* 
FROM publication p
WHERE p.pid IN (
    SELECT w1.pid
    FROM writes w1
    JOIN author a1 ON w1.aid = a1.aid
    WHERE a1.name = 'H. V. Jagadish'
    INTERSECT
    SELECT w2.pid
    FROM writes w2
    JOIN author a2 ON w2.aid = a2.aid
    WHERE a2.name = 'Divesh Srivastava'
);

data.original_question: return me the papers written by " H. V. Jagadish " and " Yunyao Li " after 2005 .
data.resolved_question: return me the papers written by " H. V. Jagadish " and " Yunyao Li " after 2005 .
data.generated_sql:
SELECT p.paperId, p.title 
FROM paper p 
JOIN writes w1 ON p.paperId = w1.paperId 
JOIN author a1 ON w1.authorId = a1.authorId 
JOIN writes w2 ON p.paperId = w2.paperId 
JOIN author a2 ON w2.authorId = a2.authorId 
WHERE a1.authorName = 'H. V. Jagadish' AND a2.authorName = 'Yunyao Li' AND p.year > 2005

data.original_question: return me the papers written by " H. V. Jagadish " and " Yunyao Li " on PVLDB .
data.resolved_question: return me the papers written by " H. V. Jagadish " and " Yunyao Li " on PVLDB .
data.generated_sql:
SELECT p.Id, p.Title, p.Year, j.ShortName AS JournalShortName
FROM Paper p
JOIN Journal j ON p.JournalId = j.Id
INNER JOIN (
    SELECT pa.PaperId
    FROM PaperAuthor pa
    JOIN Author a ON pa.AuthorId = a.Id
    WHERE a.Name = 'H. V. Jagadish'
) AS pa1 ON p.Id = pa1.PaperId
INNER JOIN (
    SELECT pa.PaperId
    FROM PaperAuthor pa
    JOIN Author a ON pa.AuthorId = a.Id
    WHERE a.Name = 'Yunyao Li'
) AS pa2 ON p.Id = pa2.PaperId
WHERE j.ShortName = 'PVLDB'

data.original_question: return me the papers written by " H. V. Jagadish " and " Yunyao Li " on PVLDB after 2005 .
data.resolved_question: return me the papers written by " H. V. Jagadish " and " Yunyao Li " on PVLDB after 2005 .
data.generated_sql:
SELECT p.paperId, p.title
FROM paper p
JOIN venue v ON p.venueId = v.venueId
JOIN writes w1 ON p.paperId = w1.paperId
JOIN author a1 ON w1.authorId = a1.authorId
JOIN writes w2 ON p.paperId = w2.paperId
JOIN author a2 ON w2.authorId = a2.authorId
WHERE a1.authorName = 'H. V. Jagadish'
AND a2.authorName = 'Yunyao Li'
AND v.venueName = 'PVLDB'
AND p.year > 2005;

data.original_question: return me the authors who have cooperated with " H. V. Jagadish " .
data.resolved_question: return me the authors who have cooperated with " H. V. Jagadish " .
data.generated_sql: SELECT DISTINCT a.authID, a.fname, a.lname FROM Authors a JOIN Authorship ON a.authID = Authorship.authID WHERE Authorship.paperID IN (SELECT paperID FROM Authorship WHERE authID = (SELECT authID FROM Authors WHERE lname = 'Jagadish' AND fname = 'H. V.')) AND a.authID != (SELECT authID FROM Authors WHERE lname = 'Jagadish' AND fname = 'H. V.')

data.original_question: return me the papers written by " H. V. Jagadish " and " Divesh Srivastava " before 2000 .
data.resolved_question: return me the papers written by " H. V. Jagadish " and " Divesh Srivastava " before 2000 .
data.generated_sql: SELECT p.* FROM paper p WHERE p.paperId IN (SELECT w.paperId FROM writes w JOIN author a ON w.authorId = a.authorId WHERE a.authorName IN ('H. V. Jagadish','Divesh Srivastava') GROUP BY w.paperId HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT a.authorId)=2) AND p.year < 2000;

data.original_question: return me the authors who have cited the papers by " H. V. Jagadish " .
data.resolved_question: return me the authors who have cited the papers by " H. V. Jagadish " .
data.generated_sql:
SELECT DISTINCT a.name
FROM author a
JOIN writes w ON a.aid = w.aid
JOIN cite c ON w.pid = c.citing
JOIN writes w2 ON c.cited = w2.pid
JOIN author a2 ON w2.aid = a2.aid
WHERE a2.name = ' H. V. Jagadish ';

data.original_question: return me the number of papers written by " H. V. Jagadish " and " Divesh Srivastava " .
data.resolved_question: return me the number of papers written by " H. V. Jagadish " and " Divesh Srivastava " .
data.generated_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM (SELECT w1.paperId FROM writes w1 JOIN author a1 ON w1.authorId = a1.authorId WHERE a1.authorName = 'H. V. Jagadish' INTERSECT SELECT w2.paperId FROM writes w2 JOIN author a2 ON w2.authorId = a2.authorId WHERE a2.authorName = 'Divesh Srivastava')
